actionPlan.iaAdjustTacDetail: TAC {current} mg/L target {target} mg/L. Do this BEFORE pH.
actionPlan.iaLowerPhDetail: pH {current} target {target}. Essential before any chlorine treatment.
actionPlan.iaPhOkDetail: pH {ph} in the ideal range. Leave it alone.
actionPlan.iaChlorineShockDetail: Free chlorine {chlorine} mg/L too low. Shock treatment (after pH balanced).
actionPlan.iaAddSlowChlorineDetail: Free chlorine {chlorine} mg/L slightly low. Top up with slow chlorine.
actionPlan.iaTreatChloraminesDetail: Combined chlorine {combined} mg/L high. Shock treatment to break down chloramines (odor, irritation).
actionPlan.iaDiluteWaterDetail: CYA {cya} mg/L blocks chlorine. Renew 20-30% of the water.
actionPlan.iaAddSaltDetail: Salt {salt} g/L too low for the electrolyzer.
actionPlan.iaTreatPhosphatesDetail: Phosphates {phosphates} mg/L: feeds algae. Use a phosphate remover.
actionPlan.iaMaintainFiltrationHours: Filter at least {hours}h to distribute products well.
actionPlan.iaMaintainFiltrationNormal: Normal filtration (half the water temperature in hours).
actionPlan.iaRetestHours: Retest in {hours}h to check the effect.
actionPlan.dndNoMixChemicals: Never mix two different chemical products (chlorine + acid = toxic gas).
actionPlan.dndNoPurePour: Never pour pure product directly into the pool without dilution (except salt).
actionPlan.dndWaterIntoAcid: Never add water to an acid product: always add the product to the water.
actionPlan.dndNoShockWithoutPh: Never do a shock chlorination without checking the pH first.
actionPlan.dndNoBathAfterShock: Never swim during or right after a shock treatment.
actionPlan.dndNoStoreChlorineAcid: Never store chlorine and acid side by side.
actionPlan.dndNoCyaOver50: Do not exceed 50 mg/L of stabilizer (CYA): chlorine becomes ineffective.
actionPlan.dndNoBath8h: Do not swim for at least 8h after the shock.
actionPlan.dndNoMaskChlorineSmell: Do not mask the chlorine smell with perfume: it's a sign of chloramines.
actionPlan.dndNoAddStabilizer: Do not add stabilizer: the level is already too high.
actionPlan.diagBalanced: Your water is generally balanced (index {cwi}/100). Maintain the testing and filtration pace. Swimming: {swim}.
actionPlan.diagIssues: Diagnosis ({sevLabel}) clear water index {cwi}/100. Issues to address: {issues}. Follow the ordered action plan below. Swimming: {swim}.
actionPlan.proPhExtreme: Extreme pH: a professional can help rebalance safely.
actionPlan.proOverChlorination: Massive over-chlorination: consider partial dilution or a pro.
actionPlan.proHighChloramines: Very high chloramines: shock needed, a pro can manage the operation.
actionPlan.dosePhMinusMethod: Dilute in a bucket of pool water, distribute in front of return jets, filtration running.
actionPlan.dosePhMinusWarningGap: Gap too large: treatment limited to -{delta} pH. Retest after filtration.
actionPlan.dosePhPlusMethod: Dilute in a bucket, distribute in front of return jets, filtration running.
actionPlan.dosePhPlusWarningGap: Gap too large: treatment limited to +{delta} pH.
actionPlan.doseChlorineShockMethod: Dissolve in a bucket of water, pour in front of return jets. Prefer evening, no swimmers.
actionPlan.doseChlorineShockWarningBath: WARNING: shock chlorination. No swimming for at least 8h.
actionPlan.doseChlorineShockWarningMix: Never mix chlorine shock and pH-.
actionPlan.doseChlorineSlowMethod: Place in skimmer or dispenser. Renew as needed.
actionPlan.doseChlorineSlowWarningSkimmer: Do not place in skimmer if simultaneous anti-algae treatment.
actionPlan.doseAlkalinityPlusMethod: Dilute, distribute, filtration running. Wait before adjusting pH.
actionPlan.doseCalciumPlusMethod: Dilute in a bucket, pour slowly in front of return jets.
actionPlan.doseStabilizerPlusMethod: Pour slowly into the skimmer, filtration running. Slow dissolution (24-48h).
actionPlan.doseStabilizerPlusWarningMax: Do not exceed 50 mg/L. Too much stabilizer blocks chlorine.
actionPlan.doseSaltPlusMethod: Pour salt directly into the pool, filtration running until fully dissolved.
actionPlan.doseSaltPlusWarningCheck: Check the salt required by your electrolyzer before adding.
actionPlan.doseAntiAlgaeMethod: Pour in front of return jets, filtration running. Brush the walls.
actionPlan.doseAntiAlgaeWarningPh: pH must be balanced BEFORE. Effective mainly as prevention.
actionPlan.doseFlocculantMethod: Pour in front of return jets, filtration 1h then stop 12h for settling, then vacuum.
actionPlan.doseFlocculantWarningFilter: Compatible with sand filter only. Do not use with cartridge filter.

emergency.title: I have a problem
emergency.subtitle: Select your situation: AQWELIA provides a guided protocol and can prepare a question for the AI assistant.
emergency.disclaimer: In case of electrical danger, leak, severe irritation or inability to treat, contact a professional.

green_water.label: Green water
green_water.title: Green water — anti-algae protocol
green_water.prompt: My water turned green, help me identify the cause and treat it step by step.
green_water.photoHint: Photograph the water surface and a wall to confirm algae presence.
steps: Check pH (target 7.0–7.4): if out of range, adjust first.
Check free chlorine: often too low (< 1 mg/L).
Check CYA (stabilizer): if > 60, chlorine is locked → dilute.
Brush walls and floor to dislodge algae.
Shock chlorine treatment after pH balanced.

cloudy_water.label: Cloudy water
cloudy_water.title: Cloudy water — clarity diagnostic
cloudy_water.prompt: My water is cloudy but not green. Help me find the cause and clear it.
cloudy_water.photoHint: Photograph the surface water and the filter pressure gauge.
steps: Measure pH (high pH clouds the water).
Check free and combined chlorine (chloramines = cloudiness).
Check CH (hardness): low CH can make water milky.
If clogged filter: backwash or flocculant.

after_storm.label: After a storm
after_storm.title: After a storm — restoration
after_storm.prompt: My pool just went through a storm (rain, heat, debris). Help me restore it.
after_storm.photoHint: Photograph the surface to check debris and color.
steps: Check and empty the pump prefilter basket.
Measure free chlorine (often diluted by rain).

back_from_vacation.label: Back from vacation
back_from_vacation.title: Back from vacation — restart
back_from_vacation.prompt: I'm back from vacation, my pool ran for several weeks without maintenance. How do I restart it?
back_from_vacation.photoHint: Photograph the water and pool floor.

chlorine_smell.label: Strong chlorine smell
chlorine_smell.title: Strong smell = chloramines
chlorine_smell.prompt: My pool smells strongly of chlorine, yet free chlorine is normal. What's going on?
steps: Measure free AND total chlorine (combined = total - free).
If combined chlorine > 0.4 mg/L: chloramines are the culprit.
DO NOT add slow chlorine: it makes it worse.
Shock treat to break down chloramines.

stinging_eyes.label: Stinging eyes
stinging_eyes.title: Irritations — pH or chloramines?
stinging_eyes.prompt: Swimmers have stinging eyes and skin irritation. Is it pH or chlorine?
steps: If pH out of range: adjust first.
If free chlorine > 4: wait for it to drop, don't swim.

wall_algae.label: Algae on walls
wall_algae.title: Algae on walls
wall_algae.prompt: I have algae forming on the pool walls. How do I eliminate it permanently?

unstable_ph.label: Unstable pH
unstable_ph.title: Unstable pH — adjust TA
unstable_ph.prompt: My pH won't hold, it keeps going up or down. How do I stabilize it?
steps: Measure alkalinity (TA): it's what stabilizes pH.
If TA < 80: add TA+ to reach 100-120 mg/L.
Wait 24h after TA adjustment before touching pH.
Check aeration / waterfall: strong stirring raises pH.

zero_chlorine.label: Chlorine at zero
zero_chlorine.title: Chlorine at zero — disinfection
zero_chlorine.prompt: My free chlorine is at 0 mg/L. How do I bring it back up safely?
steps: Check pH: if > 7.6, chlorine is ineffective.
Add slow chlorine to maintain residual.
Check CYA: if < 30, sun destroys chlorine.
No swimming until free chlorine > 1 mg/L.

too_much_chlorine.label: Too much chlorine
too_much_chlorine.title: Over-chlorination — lower chlorine
too_much_chlorine.prompt: My free chlorine is too high (> 5 mg/L). How do I bring it down?
steps: DO NOT add chlorine or stabilizer.
Covering the pool doesn't speed up the drop: leave it open.
If not urgent: wait (sun degrades free chlorine in 24-48h).

filter_high_pressure.label: Filter high pressure
filter_high_pressure.title: High filter pressure
filter_high_pressure.prompt: My filter gauge shows high pressure. What should I do?
steps: Backwash: 2-3 minutes until water runs clear.
Rinse 30 seconds to reset the sand.
If pressure stays high: filter media may need replacing.
Check that valves are fully open.
Note the date of last wash for tracking.

electrolyzer_error.label: Electrolyzer error
electrolyzer_error.title: Electrolyzer error
electrolyzer_error.prompt: My salt electrolyzer shows an error code. What are the possible causes?
electrolyzer_error.photoHint: Photograph the electrolyzer display and the cell.
steps: Measure salt: if < 4 g/L, the cell doesn't produce.
Inspect the cell: if scaled, descale in diluted acid.
Consult the manual for the exact code.

startup.label: Restart
startup.title: Restart (spring)
startup.prompt: It's spring, I'm restarting my pool after winter. Give me the full protocol.
steps: Remove the winter cover, clean and dry it.
Reconnect the pump and set valves to summer position.
Check water level, top up if needed.
Measure pH, chlorine, TA, CYA, salt.
Adjust pH, then TA, then shock treat.

winterizing.label: Winterizing
winterizing.title: Pool winterizing
winterizing.prompt: I want to winterize my pool properly for winter. Give me the active/passive protocol.
steps: Lower water level below skimmers (10-15 cm).
Drain pipes, install gizmos and floats.
Turn off pump (passive) or keep running (active, > 0°C).

landing.heroTitle: Pool maintenance has never been easier
landing.heroSubtitle: Analyze, Understand, Act. AQWELIA takes care of your pool.
landing.problemTitle: Pool maintenance is a nightmare for most owners.
landing.problemGreenWaterText: You wake up, the water is green. Panic. What to do? In what order?
landing.problemStormText: Chlorine has dissolved, pH has shifted. Risky to swim?
landing.problemEyesText: “Too much chlorine,” you think. Wrong: it's chloramines.
landing.problemOverdoseText: You pour by guesswork. 30% of products go down the drain.
landing.problemVacationText: 2 weeks unsupervised = 1 week of heavy treatment.
landing.problemQuote: “We spend more time Googling than enjoying the pool.”
landing.costsTitle: How much does your pool REALLY cost each year?
landing.costsSubtitle: People massively underestimate the real cost. Here are French and European averages (sources: FPP, Aquaguard, 2023-2024 market studies).
landing.costsRow2Note: Often ON TOP of the pro
landing.costsCalloutText: green water (€80 of products + 2 days), electrolyzer breakdown (€150 pro call-out), leak (€300-2000).
landing.piscinisteTitle: The pool pro is useful. But doesn't solve everything.
landing.piscinisteNotIncludedTitle: What the pool pro does NOT do
landing.piscinisteNotIncluded1: Be there on Sunday when the water turns
landing.piscinisteNotIncluded2: Watch between visits (storm Tuesday, visit Friday)
landing.piscinisteNotIncluded3: Tell you “RIGHT NOW, can I swim?”
landing.piscinisteNotIncluded6: Teach you to understand your pool
landing.piscinisteCallout1: With a pool pro, you're on your own
landing.solutionStep1Text: Take a photo of the water / filter / test strip, enter your values (or scan).
landing.solutionStep2Text: AI + deterministic engine analyze: pool profile, weather, history.
landing.solutionStep3Text: 1. TA+ 2040g → 2. pH- 900ml → 3. Shock chlorine → filtration 4h → re-test 3h → no swimming 8h.
landing.solutionDeterministText1: We don't trust your safety to an LLM. Calculations are exact, testable, prudent.
landing.comparatorFeature2: AI photo diagnostic (water, filter, electrolyzer, cell)
landing.comparatorConclusion1: No app combines a deterministic engine + multimodal VLM + real-time weather + contextual reminders.
landing.simulationsTitle: What AQWELIA changes, scene by scene.
landing.simulationsS1Without: Panic. Pool pro unreachable. Random purchase of algaecide + flocculant (€40). Water still cloudy Monday. Swimming cancelled.
landing.simulationsS1With: Photo → AI detects algae + likely high pH. Plan: pH- (€4.5) → measured shock chlorine → brush → filter 24h. Cost: €12. Clear water Monday.
landing.simulationsS2Without: No idea. Storm passes. Water unbalanced for 2 days. Risky swimming.
landing.simulationsS2With: Weather alert “Storm 70% tonight → check chlorine before 8pm”. Proactive action. Stable water.
landing.simulationsS3Without: Green or even black water. Pool out of service for 1 week. €80 of products + 2 days of work.
landing.simulationsS3With: Before leaving → “Vacation mode” guide (shock, full dispenser, scheduled filtration, cover). Return: clear water, 15-min check.
landing.simulationsS4Without: “Too much chlorine” → cut chlorine → green water 3 days later.
landing.simulationsS4With: AQWELIA explains “chloramines” (combined chlorine). Recommends a shock to break them. Right diagnosis, right action.
landing.simulationsS5Without: Water turns. Algae. Chlorine overuse. Family conflict.
landing.simulationsS5With: Alert “Heatwave → +2h filtration + daily chlorine test”. Prevention. Stable water.
landing.simulationsS6Without: Wait for the pool pro's visit. Cloudy water meanwhile.
landing.simulationsS6With: Photo of the gauge → “High pressure, backwash recommended” + step-by-step guide. Immediate, free action.
landing.simulationsNote: Based on real cases experienced by the founders.
landing.savingsRoi3: And you get 25h of free time back.
landing.storyTitle: Built by pool owners, for pool owners.
landing.storyQuote1: “Three roots, one promise: water done right, powered by AI.”
landing.storyQuote2: “After 15 years, 3 different pools (concrete, shell, above-ground), 2 electrolyzers, a sand filter and a cartridge filter, hundreds of tests, dozens of bad surprises — we'd had enough.”
landing.storyQuote3: “We tried everything: the expensive pool pro who comes once a week, basic apps that say ‘add chlorine’, forums, YouTube videos at 11pm.”
landing.storyQuote4a: “Nothing answered the only question that matters:
landing.storyQuote4b: “RIGHT NOW, with MY readings, MY pool, MY weather, what do I do, in what order, and when can I swim?”
landing.storyQuote5: “AQWELIA was born from that frustration. Not by a team of marketers. By avid users sick of green water.”
landing.variationsTitle: Why does water change all the time?
landing.variationsFactor1Text: Storms (dilute chlorine, shift pH), heatwaves (algae), wind (debris), frost (equipment).
landing.variationsFactor2Text: More swimmers = more organic matter = chlorine consumed faster.
landing.variationsFactor3Text: Phosphates and oily residue → cloudy water, algae.
landing.variationsFactor5Text: Chlorine evaporates faster when hot, chemical activity increases.
landing.variationsFactor8Text: Filter loading up, electrolyzer scaling, pump aging.
landing.variationsCallout2: these variations to tailor its advice.
landing.featuresItem1Text: Clear water index, swimming safety, daily actions.
landing.featuresItem2Text: Water, filter, electrolyzer, test strip (prudent VLM).
landing.featuresItem3Text: pH, chlorine, TA, CH, CYA, salt, phosphates + auto plan.
landing.featuresItem4Text: Knows your pool and your history.
landing.internationalSubtitle: 7 languages, 10 countries, standards adapted to your region. AQWELIA adapts to you, wherever you are.
landing.internationalLangDesc: Choose your language, regardless of your country of residence.
landing.internationalCountriesDesc: pH, chlorine, bromine thresholds and units (°C/°F, L/gal) adjust automatically based on your country.
landing.internationalNormsDesc: pH, chlorine, bromine: standards vary by country. AQWELIA adapts automatically (France DGS, USA CDC, Germany DIN 19643, UK PWTAG...)
landing.internationalLanguageDesc: A Mexican in the USA can use the app in Spanish. Language is independent from country of residence.
landing.internationalUnitsDesc: Metric or imperial, °C or °F, m³ or gallons. AQWELIA adapts to your habits, not the other way around.
landing.internationalMarketplaceText: Test strips, chemicals, equipment: AQWELIA recommends the best sellers in your country (Amazon.fr, Leslie's USA, Poolstore UK...).
landing.spaSubtitle: Hot water, small volume, many bathers: a spa is not a pool. Specific treatments (bromine, active oxygen), regular draining, covering and pump programs — AQWELIA handles everything.
landing.spaCard1Text: Bromine or active oxygen — no chlorine that evaporates in hot water. AQWELIA guides you to the right disinfectant based on your spa's temperature.
landing.spaCard2Text: Drain reminders calculated based on your usage. Draining every 3-4 months is often MORE economical than saturating the water with products.
landing.spaCard3Text: Cover after each bath: anti-evaporation, anti-algae, heat retention. AQWELIA reminds you of the right daily habits.
landing.spaCard4Text: Jacuzzi, Sundance, Hot Spring, Bestway, Intex, Wellis, Desjoyaux… or generic Chinese. AQWELIA recognizes your spa and adapts its advice.
landing.spaCard5Text: Number of seats, usage frequency, pump programs, target temperature. AQWELIA calculates drains and filtration cycles based on YOUR reality.
landing.spaPremiumText1: Full Spa support (bromine, active oxygen, smart draining, pump programs) is reserved for the
landing.spaTreatmentsDesc: At high temperature, the choice of disinfectant changes everything.
landing.spaDrainageTitle: Drain rather than over-treat: the economic calculation
landing.spaDrainageText1: After 3-4 months, spa water is saturated with organic matter, minerals and treatment residue. Continuing to add products becomes
landing.spaDrainageText3: than a full drain. AQWELIA alerts you at the right time and calculates the ideal frequency based on your usage.
landing.spaBrandsDesc: AQWELIA recognizes the main global brands and generic Chinese spas.
landing.faqQ1: Does AQWELIA replace the pool professional?
landing.faqA1: No. It complements them. The pool pro visits once a week; AQWELIA covers the other 6 days, handles emergencies, and teaches you to understand your pool. Many users keep a pool pro + AQWELIA.
landing.faqA2: The AI is cautious and always displays a confidence level. Critical dosage calculations are NOT done by the AI but by a deterministic engine. In case of doubt, AQWELIA says "consult a professional".
landing.faqQ3: Can I use AQWELIA without a pool pro?
landing.faqA3: Yes, that's the most common case. The app guides you step by step like a good pool pro would.
landing.faqA4: Yes, 100%. No resale. Your photos and measurements remain yours.
landing.faqA5: The web mode requires a connection. The native mobile app will have an offline mode for tests and logs.
landing.faqA6: Yes! AQWELIA manages spas with dedicated features: bromine or active oxygen treatment, temperature monitoring, drain reminders, anti-evaporation covering, adapted filtration programs. Available from the Lagoon plan.
landing.faqQ7: What if I have multiple pools?
landing.faqA7: The Lagoon plan allows 1 pool + 1 spa, the Atlas plan is unlimited (for pool pros).
landing.faqA8: Yes, no commitment. No hidden fees.
landing.finalCtaTitle: Your pool deserves a real copilot.
landing.finalCtaSubtitle: Free to start. No credit card. In 2 minutes you'll know what to do.
landing.finalCtaDisclaimer: Already 11 modules · 20 guides · deterministic dosage engine · real-time weather
landing.disclaimer: AQWELIA assists with diagnosis and maintenance but does not replace a professional. Dosages must follow product instructions. In case of doubt, electrical danger, leak or irritation, contact a professional.
landing.footerTagline: The AI copilot that tells you exactly what to do, how much, in what order, and when to swim. Made in France 🇫🇷.

green-water.title: Green water: diagnosis and action plan
green-water.summary: Identify the cause of green water and treat it step by step without overdosing.

1.title: Measure pH and free chlorine
1.detail: Green water is almost always due to a lack of disinfectant or unbalanced pH.
1.warning: Never add anti-algae before balancing the pH.

2.title: Balance the pH (7.0-7.4)
2.detail: If pH > 7.4, add diluted pH-. Wait 2-3h of filtration.
2.tip: Chlorine is 10x more effective at pH 7.2 than at pH 8.0.

3.title: Brush the walls and floor
3.detail: Dislodging algae helps the treatment penetrate.

4.title: Do a shock treatment
4.detail: Shock chlorine (after pH balanced). Continuous filtration 24h.
4.warning: No swimming for at least 8h after the shock.

5.title: Add a curative anti-algae
5.detail: After the shock, anti-algae prevents regrowth.

6.title: Filter continuously 24-48h
6.detail: Filtration is as important as chemicals.

7.title: Re-test after 24h
7.detail: Check pH and chlorine. If the water is still green, the filter may need cleaning.

cloudy-water.title: Cloudy or milky water
cloudy-water.summary: Distinguish the causes of cloudy water and apply the right treatment.

1.title: Check the filtration
1.detail: 80% of cloudy water comes from a filtration problem. Check pressure, duration, filter cleanliness.

2.title: Test pH and chlorine
2.detail: Out-of-range pH or low chlorine = cloudy water.

3.title: Clean or backwash the filter
3.detail: Clogged filter = cloudy water.

4.title: Use a flocculant (if sand filter)
4.detail: Flocculant clumps fine particles so the filter can catch them.
4.warning: Flocculant only with sand filter. Not with cartridge.

5.title: Filter 24h then vacuum deposits
5.detail: After flocculation, deposits form at the bottom: vacuum them up.

chlorine-shock.title: When and how to do a chlorine shock
chlorine-shock.summary: Shock treatment: indications, dosage, safety, swim delays.

1.title: Check pH BEFORE
1.detail: A shock is ineffective if pH > 7.6. Balance first.
1.warning: NEVER shock without checking pH first.

2.title: Choose the right time
2.detail: In the evening, no swimmers, filtration running.

3.title: Dose according to volume
3.detail: AQWELIA calculates the amount based on your basin volume.

4.title: Dissolve in a bucket
4.detail: Never pour powder shock directly at the edge.

6.title: Wait 8h before swimming
6.detail: Check free chlorine before swimming (must be < 3 mg/L).

combined-chlorine.title: Strong chlorine smell and stinging eyes
combined-chlorine.summary: Understand chloramines and how to get rid of them.

1.title: Understand the cause
1.detail: Strong chlorine smell is NOT excess chlorine: it is chloramines (combined chlorine), disinfection by-products.

2.title: Measure BOTH free and total chlorine
2.detail: Combined chlorine = total chlorine - free chlorine. If > 0.4 mg/L, treatment needed.

3.title: Do a shock treatment
3.detail: Shock breaks down chloramines and releases active free chlorine.

4.title: Check the pH
4.detail: High pH worsens chloramines.

filter-backwash.title: Sand filter backwash (counter-rinse)
filter-backwash.summary: When and how to do an effective backwash.

1.title: When to backwash?
1.detail: When pressure rises 0.3-0.5 bar above service pressure, or every 2-4 weeks.

2.title: Turn off the pump
2.detail: ALWAYS turn off the pump before handling the valve.

3.title: Set valve to BACKWASH
3.detail: Turn the multi-port valve firmly.

4.title: Turn the pump back on 2-3 min
4.detail: Water flows to the drain until it becomes clear again.

5.title: Turn off, valve to RINSE 30s
5.detail: Rinsing repacks the sand and prevents dirt return.

6.title: Return to FILTER position
6.detail: Set the valve back to normal filtration.

filter-cartridge-clean.title: Clean a cartridge filter
filter-cartridge-clean.summary: Disassembly and rinsing of a filter cartridge.

1.title: Turn off the pump
1.detail: Cut the power supply.

2.title: Remove the cartridge
2.detail: Open the filter body according to the model.

3.title: Rinse with a jet
3.detail: Gentle water jet, top to bottom, between the folds.

4.title: Soak in a filter cleaner (optional)
4.detail: Every 2-3 cleanings, degreasing and descaling.

cell-clean.title: Clean the electrolyzer cell
cell-clean.summary: Descale the salt electrolyzer cell to maintain chlorine production.

1.title: Cut the power
1.detail: Turn off the electrolyzer and the pump.
1.warning: Electrical safety. Never handle the cell under power.

2.title: Remove the cell
2.detail: Unscrew according to the model. Check the electrodes.

3.title: Prepare a diluted acid solution
3.detail: 1/3 hydrochloric acid + 2/3 water in a plastic container.
3.warning: ALWAYS pour acid into water, never the reverse. Gloves and goggles mandatory. Ventilation.

4.title: Soak 10-15 min
4.detail: Scale dissolves. Do not leave too long.

5.title: Rinse thoroughly
5.detail: With clean water until the smell disappears.

salt-system-basics.title: Understand your salt electrolyzer
salt-system-basics.summary: Principle, salt level, production, routine maintenance.

1.title: The principle
1.detail: Salt (NaCl) is electrolyzed into active chlorine in-situ. No chlorine storage, softer water.

2.title: Required salt level
2.detail: Depending on the model: generally 3-7 g/L. Check the manual. Too low = no production; too high = overconsumption.

3.title: Stabilizer (CYA) essential
3.detail: Without stabilizer, the produced chlorine is destroyed by the sun in 2h.

4.title: Monitor the pH
4.detail: The electrolyzer raises the pH. Frequent regulation needed.

5.title: Production by temperature
5.detail: In cold water (< 15°C), production drops. Do not worry in winter.

6.title: Cell maintenance
6.detail: Acid cleaning every 3-6 months depending on water hardness.

ph-control.title: Master your pH
ph-control.summary: Why pH is the #1 parameter, how to adjust it.

1.title: pH is the priority
1.detail: Everything starts with pH: chlorine effectiveness, swim comfort, equipment protection.

2.title: Ideal range 7.0-7.4
2.detail: Closer to 7.2 = optimal chlorine.

3.title: Adjust TAC before pH if unstable
3.detail: A low TAC makes pH unstable.

4.title: Dose in small amounts
4.detail: Never change pH by more than 0.3 at once.

tac-control.title: Understand alkalinity (TAC)
tac-control.summary: TAC is the pH buffer. Without good TAC, pH becomes unstable.

1.title: Role of TAC
1.detail: TAC (total alkalinity) stabilizes pH. Without it, pH jumps with every chemical addition.

2.title: Ideal range 80-120 mg/L
2.detail: Target 100 mg/L.

3.title: Adjust BEFORE pH
3.detail: Always balance TAC first, otherwise pH will not hold.

4.title: To raise: sodium bicarbonate
4.detail: Dilute, distribute, filtration 3h.

5.title: To lower: acid
5.detail: More complex, done slowly with a pH-.

after-storm.title: What to do after a storm?
after-storm.summary: Lightning dilutes chlorine and changes pH. Post-storm routine.

1.title: Test pH and chlorine
1.detail: Storms often acidify water and dilute chlorine.

2.title: Clean skimmer and basket
2.detail: Debris brought by rain and wind.

3.title: Adjust pH if necessary
3.detail: Priority to pH before any chlorine addition.

4.title: Top up chlorine if low
4.detail: Slow or shock chlorine depending on the gap.

vacation-mode.title: Before going on vacation
vacation-mode.summary: Prepare your pool for an absence of several days/weeks.

1.title: Do a full test
1.detail: Balance everything before leaving: pH, chlorine, TAC.

2.title: Shock chlorine the day before
2.detail: Disinfect deeply.

3.title: Fill the slow chlorine dispenser
3.detail: For an absence < 1 week.

4.title: Set the filtration timer
4.detail: At least 4-6h/day, preferably at night.

5.title: Cover the pool
5.detail: Tarp or cover: limits debris and evaporation.

6.title: For a long absence: neighbor or robot
6.detail: Have someone check or install a robot.

7.title: Depending on season: active or passive winterization
7.detail: In summer, reduced filtration is enough. In winter, full winterization.

winterization.title: Winterize your pool
winterization.summary: Put the pool into winterization without damaging equipment.

1.title: Choose active or passive winterization
1.detail: Active: reduced filtration. Passive: full stop. Depends on climate.

2.title: Deep clean
2.detail: Water, walls, floor, filter, water line.

3.title: Do a winter treatment
3.detail: Winterizing product, anti-algae and anti-scale.

4.title: Lower the water level
4.detail: 10-15 cm below the jets for passive.

startup.title: Spring start-up
startup.summary: Wake up your pool after winter gently.

1.title: Remove the cover and clean
1.detail: Remove accumulated leaves and debris.

2.title: Refill if water level is low
2.detail: Restore the level.

3.title: Start the filtration
3.detail: Check the filter (backwash if needed).

4.title: Test the water
4.detail: pH, chlorine, TAC, stabilizer.

6.title: Do a shock treatment
6.detail: To start on a healthy base.

swim-safety.title: Swim safety: when can you swim?

1.title: pH between 7.0 and 7.6
1.detail: Outside this range: possible irritation.

2.title: Free chlorine between 1 and 3 mg/L
2.detail: Less = insufficient disinfection. More = irritation.

3.title: Combined chlorine < 0.4 mg/L
3.detail: Above: strong smell, stinging eyes.

4.title: Respect delays after treatment
4.detail: 2h after pH, 8h after chlorine shock.

5.title: If in doubt: do not swim
5.detail: Better to wait for a test than to go into questionable water.

product-safety.title: Chemical product safety
product-safety.summary: Golden rules for handling and storing pool products.

1.title: NEVER mix products
1.detail: Chlorine + acid = deadly toxic gas. Always dilute separately.

2.title: Always add product to water
2.detail: Never water into acid product (splashing).

3.title: Wear gloves and goggles
3.detail: Especially for acids and concentrated chlorine.

4.title: Store dry, cool, ventilated
4.detail: Separate acids and chlorine. Out of reach of children.

5.title: Follow the instructions
5.detail: Each product has its dosage and application rules.

6.title: In case of incident: call emergency services or poison control
6.detail: Do not induce vomiting, consult immediately.

getting-started.title: Getting started with your pool
getting-started.summary: The first 10 things to know when you become a pool owner.

1.title: Know your pool volume
1.detail: Essential for any dosage. AQWELIA calculates it from dimensions.

2.title: Understand pH
2.detail: The #1 parameter. Everything starts here.

3.title: Understand chlorine
3.detail: Free, total, combined. AQWELIA guides you.

4.title: Filter enough
4.detail: Half the water temperature in hours.

7.title: Monitor the water level
7.detail: Too low = pump at risk; too high = inefficient skimmer.

8.title: Brush and clean the water line

9.title: Anticipate the weather
9.detail: Storm, heatwave, vacation: prevent rather than cure.

10.title: Keep a log
10.detail: AQWELIA keeps the history for you.

getting-started-spa.title: Spa specifics
getting-started-spa.summary: The spa has its own rules: hot water, reduced volume, bromine over chlorine.

1.title: Reduced volume = fast reactions
1.detail: Water turns quickly. More frequent tests.

2.title: Prefer bromine over chlorine
2.detail: Bromine is more stable when hot and less odorous.

3.title: Temperature and pH
3.detail: pH changes with temperature. Test at bathing temperature.

4.title: Weekly shock
4.detail: More necessary than in a pool.

faq-test-frequency.title: How often should you test your water?
faq-test-frequency.summary: Recommended frequency by season and usage.

1.title: In warm season
1.detail: At least twice a week, more in hot weather.

2.title: In cold season (active winterization)
2.detail: Once every 2 weeks.

3.title: After any treatment
3.detail: Re-test within 3-24h depending on product.

4.title: After a storm
4.detail: Systematic test.

faq-cya-high.title: My stabilizer (CYA) is too high
faq-cya-high.summary: What to do when CYA exceeds 50-60 mg/L?

1.title: Why it's a problem
1.detail: Too-high CYA "locks" chlorine: it is present but inactive. Algae risk despite normal chlorine.

2.title: There is no product to lower CYA
2.detail: Only solution: dilute the water.

3.title: Renew 20-30% of the water
3.detail: Partial drain then top up.

4.title: Re-test after renewal
4.detail: Check the new value.

5.title: In future: use less stabilized chlorine

diagnosticActionPlan.waterTestNote: Measurements are saved in your logbook (

diagnosticActionPlan.genericIntro1: The AI identified a problem but no typical pattern (green / cloudy water). Follow the recommendation below.

diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction3: Adjust if necessary with pH- or pH+.
diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction4: Record your measurement below for history.
diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction5: Check that filtration runs at least 8h/day.
diagnosticActionPlan.genericIntro2: After applying the recommendations, take a new photo to confirm resolution.
diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction2_1: Wait at least 24h of continuous filtration.

diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction2_3: Click Verify result at the bottom of the plan.
diagnosticActionPlan.genericInstruction2_4: The AI will compare and tell if the problem is resolved.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1Intro: pH must be between 7.0 and 7.4 BEFORE any treatment. An incorrect pH makes chlorine ineffective (up to -90%).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I1: Take your test kit (drops or strip) or an electronic tester.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I2: Collect a water sample at 30 cm depth, away from return jets.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I3: Measure current pH and note it below.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I4: If pH > 7.4 → add pH- (acid). If pH < 7.0 → add pH+ (base).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I5: Reference dosage: ~{qty} g of pH- to lower by 0.1 unit on {volume} m³.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I6: Dilute the product in a bucket of pool water, never pure.

diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1I8: Wait 2 hours with filtration running before re-testing.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS1Dosage: ~{qty} g of pH- / 0.1 unit to lower (pool of {volume} m³)
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS2Intro: After 2h of filtration, the pH has had time to stabilize. Check that it is now in the target range.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS2I1: Take a new water sample at 30 cm depth.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS2I3: If pH between 7.0 and 7.4 → ✅ proceed to next step (shock treatment).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS2I4: If pH still too high/low → adjust again with HALF the previous dose.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS2Product: pH- or pH+ (reduced dose if re-adjusting)

diagnosticActionPlan.greenS3Intro: Shock chlorine destroys algae, bacteria and viruses. This is the most important step against green water.

diagnosticActionPlan.greenS3I2: Calculate curative dose: about 10 g of shock chlorine per m³.

diagnosticActionPlan.greenS3I6: Pour in front of return jets, never in the skimmer.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS3I7: 🚫 NO SWIMMING for at least 8 hours.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS3Product: Shock chlorine (65% active, granules or tablets)
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4Intro: Anti-algae complements chlorine by destroying resistant algae and preventing regrowth.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4I1: Wait at least 2h after shock treatment (never mix directly).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4I2: Curative dose: ~20 mL of anti-algae per m³.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4I3: For {volume} m³: {qty} mL of anti-algae (i.e. {liters} L).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4I4: Pour directly in front of return jets, pump running.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS4I5: Brush walls right after (see next step) to dislodge algae.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5Intro: Brushing dislodges embedded algae. Without this step, chemical treatment is not enough.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I1: Use a brush suited to your surface: nylon brush for liner/shell, steel brush for concrete.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I2: 🚫 Never use a metal brush on liner or reinforced membrane — you would puncture it.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I3: Brush top to bottom, in 1 m² sections.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I4: Focus on corners, steps, jets, ladders and the water line.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I5: Also brush the floor if accessible (telescopic pole).
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS5I6: It's physical (15-30 min) but ESSENTIAL — without it algae return in 48h.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS6Intro: Filtration must run 24h without interruption to remove dead algae and particles.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS6I1: Set the pump to forced mode (24/7) — no timer program.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS6I3: If pressure rises > 0.3 bar above normal → do a backwash.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS6I4: Clean the pump pre-filter (basket) if full of debris.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS6I6: For a cartridge filter: rinse it midway if flow drops.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS7Intro: After 24h of filtration, do a full test to verify water is safe and balanced.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS7I1: Take a sample at 30 cm depth.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS7I5: Enter your 3 values below — they will be saved in your logbook.
diagnosticActionPlan.greenS7I6: If everything is in range → you can swim again. Otherwise, adjust.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1Intro: Cloudy water usually comes from ineffective filtration. Always start here.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I1: Turn off the pump before any safety intervention.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I2: Check pressure gauge: if > 0.3 bar above initial value → clean.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I3: Sand filter: backwash 2-3 min then rinse 30 s.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I4: Cartridge filter: disassemble, hose off, replace if > 2 years old.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I5: Empty and clean skimmer and pump pre-filter baskets.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS1I6: Restart and check that flow is good (return jets).
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS2Intro: An unbalanced pH promotes turbidity and reduces chlorine effectiveness.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS2I2: If pH > 7.4 → add pH-; if pH < 7.0 → add pH+.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS2I3: Reference: ~{qty} g of pH- to lower by 0.1 unit on {volume} m³.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS2I4: Dilute in a bucket of pool water, pour in front of return jets.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3Intro: Flocculant aggregates fine particles into flocs the filter can catch. ⚠ Sand filter only.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3I1: ⚠ NEVER use flocculant with a cartridge filter (clogging).
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3I2: Dose: ~5 mL of liquid flocculant per m³.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3I4: Pour in front of return jets, pump running 1h.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3I5: Then turn off filtration 12h to let it settle.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS3I6: Flocs fall to the bottom — vacuum them with robot or manual vacuum.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS4Intro: Let filtration run 12h to clarify water after flocculation/vacuuming.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS4I1: Restart pump in forced mode after vacuuming flocs.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS4I2: Monitor filter pressure — backwash if it rises.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS4I4: After 12h, water should be clear again.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS5Intro: Verify water is balanced and chlorine is active.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS5I3: Enter your values below — they will be saved in your logbook.
diagnosticActionPlan.cloudyS5I4: If values are OK and water clear → problem solved.
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae1Desc: Algae are still present despite the first treatment. Repeat shock treatment with 1.5x the previous dose. Verify pH is between 7.0-7.4 BEFORE. Prefer a different shock chlorine brand (algae can develop resistance).
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae1Reason: Algae can be resistant. A stronger dose and a different product are needed.
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae2Desc: Count the number of filtration hours per day. For green water, filter at least 12h/24. Ideally 24h/24 for 3 consecutive days.
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae2Reason: Insufficient filtration prevents removing dead algae and clarifying water.
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae3Desc: If you have a sand filter, backwash for 3 minutes followed by 30s rinse. If it's a cartridge, remove and hose it off. A clogged filter doesn't catch algae and returns them to the pool.
diagnosticActionPlan.compAlgae3Reason: The filter may be saturated with algae after the first treatment.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy1Desc: Water is still cloudy. Pour LIQUID flocculant (not sock) directly in front of return jets, pump running. Filter 1h, then turn off 12h to let settle. Particles will aggregate and fall to the bottom.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy1Reason: Flocculant aggregates fine particles the filter doesn't catch.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy2Desc: After 12h of settling, debris will be at the bottom. Vacuum slowly in 'waste' position to avoid sending them back to the filter. Only switch the valve back to filtration after vacuuming is done.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy2Reason: Flocs are too heavy for the normal filter.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy3Desc: If water stays cloudy despite flocculation + filtration: your sand/cartridge may need replacement. Sand is changed every 5-7 years, cartridge every 2 years. Worn media no longer catches fine particles.
diagnosticActionPlan.compCloudy3Reason: An end-of-life filter is a common cause of persistent cloudy water.
diagnosticActionPlan.compNewIssuesDesc: New problem(s) appeared: {issues}. Analyze the probable cause (over-chlorination, flocculant overdose, limescale precipitation, etc.) before acting. If in doubt, redo a full pH/chlorine/TA/CH test.
diagnosticActionPlan.compNewIssuesReason: Some treatments can create new imbalances. They must be corrected before they worsen the situation.
diagnosticActionPlan.compFallback1Desc: Water is improving but not perfect yet. Let filtration run 24h more continuously. Monitor filter pressure (backwash if it rises by +0.3 bar).
diagnosticActionPlan.compFallback1Reason: Treatment is in progress, give it time to work.
diagnosticActionPlan.compFallback2Desc: Redo a full test (pH, chlorine, TA) in 24h and take a new photo to verify. If satisfaction score stagnates, consider draining 20-30% of water to restart on a healthy base.
diagnosticActionPlan.compFallback2Reason: Treatment needs time to take effect before concluding failure.

cgu.metaDescription: Terms of use of the AQWELIA service.
cgu.title: Terms of Use
cgu.article1Body1: These Terms of Use ("<bold>TOU</bold>") define the terms and conditions of access to and use of the AQWELIA application ("<bold>the Service</bold>"), published by the company AQWELIA, as well as the rights and obligations of the parties in this context.
cgu.article1Body2: Use of the Service implies full and complete acceptance of these TOU. If you do not agree with any of these conditions, you must not use the Service.
cgu.article2Item1: "<bold>User</bold>": any natural person who uses the Service.
cgu.article2Item2: "<bold>Account</bold>": personal space created by the User to access the Service.
cgu.article2Item3: "<bold>Data</bold>": information entered by the User (measurements, photos, pool profile, etc.).
cgu.article2Item4: "<bold>Plan</bold>": subscription formula (Free, Premium or Expert) to which the User subscribes.
cgu.article2Item5: "<bold>Content</bold>": recommendations, action plans, analyses, guides and texts generated by the Service.
cgu.article3Body1: AQWELIA is an intelligent pool maintenance copilot. The Service allows the User to:
cgu.article3Item1: monitor and analyze the quality of their pool water (pH, chlorine, TAC, TH, etc.);
cgu.article3Item2: obtain personalized action plans (dosages, filtration, swimability);
cgu.article3Item3: visually diagnose water and equipment via photo;
cgu.article3Item4: consult an AI assistant for any maintenance question;
cgu.article3Item5: receive smart reminders (weather, equipment, product stocks);
cgu.article3Body2: The Service is provided for informational purposes and does not replace the intervention of a qualified professional (pool specialist, retailer, lifeguard).
cgu.article4Body1: Registration for the Service requires creating an Account via a valid email address and a password. The User agrees to provide accurate information and to keep it up to date.
cgu.article4Body2: The User is solely responsible for the confidentiality of their credentials and any use of their Account. Any activity performed from their Account is deemed to be performed by the User.
cgu.article4Body3: AQWELIA reserves the right to refuse or close an Account in the event of non-compliance with these TOU or fraudulent behavior.
cgu.article5Item1: use the Service in accordance with its intended purpose and applicable law;
cgu.article5Item2: not attempt to divert, disable or compromise the security of the Service;
cgu.article5Item3: not upload Content that is unlawful, defamatory or infringes the rights of third parties;
cgu.article5Item4: comply with the instructions of the chemical products used for maintaining their pool.
cgu.article5Body2: The User acknowledges that the recommendations provided by the Service are indicative and that they remain solely responsible for the actions they take on their pool.
cgu.article6Item1: <bold>Free</bold>: free, includes 1 pool, basic manual measurements, limited assistant.
cgu.article6Item2: <bold>Premium</bold>: up to 3 pools, unlimited photo diagnostic, full AI assistant, weather reminders, advanced guides.
cgu.article6Item3: <bold>Expert</bold>: unlimited (professional pool specialists), multi-client, exports, priority support.
cgu.article6Body2: The features and pricing of each Plan are detailed on the <link>Pricing</link> page. AQWELIA reserves the right to modify these offers at any time, subject to informing subscribed Users at least 30 days before entry into force.
cgu.article7Body1: Payment for Premium and Expert subscriptions is made according to the platform used:
cgu.article7Item2: <bold>iOS</bold>: via Apple In-App Purchase (managed by Apple).
cgu.article7Item3: <bold>Android</bold>: via Google Play Billing (managed by Google).
cgu.article7Body2: Renewal is automatic at the end of the subscribed period, unless deactivated by the User in their Apple/Google account settings or via the Stripe customer portal.
cgu.article8Body1: The User can cancel their subscription at any time from the application (<link>Settings</link> page) or from the App Store / Google Play settings. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.
cgu.article8Body2: AQWELIA reserves the right to terminate or suspend access to the Service in the event of a breach of the TOU, after prior notification except in an emergency.
cgu.article9Body1: The processing of the User's personal data is governed by our <link>Privacy Policy</link>, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR, EU 2016/679).
cgu.article9Body2: The User has the right of access, rectification, erasure, portability and objection. These rights can be exercised from the <link>Settings</link> page or by email at <alink>privacy@aqwelia.app</alink>.
cgu.article10Body1: AQWELIA makes every effort to ensure the availability and reliability of the Service, but cannot guarantee a total absence of errors or interruptions.
cgu.article10Body2: As the Service is provided for informational purposes, AQWELIA cannot be held liable for direct or indirect damages resulting from the application of the recommendations (including: material damage, loss of swimming, equipment deterioration, chemical reactions). In case of doubt, the User should consult a professional.
cgu.article11Body1: All elements of the Service (brand, logo, texts, algorithms, design, guides) are the exclusive property of AQWELIA or its partners. Any reproduction, representation or distribution, in whole or in part, without prior written authorization is prohibited.
cgu.article11Body2: The Data entered by the User remains their property. The User grants AQWELIA a non-exclusive license to use this Data to the extent necessary for the provision and improvement of the Service.
cgu.article12Body1: AQWELIA may modify these TOU at any time. The applicable TOU are those in force at the time of use of the Service. Substantial modifications will be notified by email or via the application at least 30 days before their entry into force.
cgu.article13Body1: These TOU are governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, the parties agree to seek an amicable solution before any legal action. Failing agreement, the French courts shall have sole jurisdiction.
cgu.contactBody1: For any question regarding these TOU: <alink>legal@aqwelia.app</alink>
cgu.contactBody2: For any question regarding your data: <alink>privacy@aqwelia.app</alink>

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Body1: The controller of the personal data collected via the AQWELIA application is the company <bold>AQWELIA</bold>, publisher of the Service.
privacy.section2Body1: The Service collects the following categories of data:
privacy.section2Item2: <bold>Pool profile</bold>: volume, shape, surface, treatment, filter, exposure, region.
privacy.section2Item3: <bold>Water data</bold>: measurements (pH, chlorine, TAC, TH, CYA, salt, temperature, etc.) entered or imported.
privacy.section2Item4: <bold>Photos</bold>: images of water, walls, filter, electrolyzers and products, used for AI visual diagnostic.
privacy.section2Item5: <bold>Equipment & inventory</bold>: brand, model, maintenance dates, chemicals in stock.
privacy.section2Item6: <bold>History</bold>: conversations with the AI assistant, generated action plans, photo diagnostics.
privacy.section2Item7: <bold>Billing data</bold>: subscribed plan, period, status (payment information is processed by Stripe, Apple or Google — AQWELIA does not store card numbers).
privacy.section2Item8: <bold>Usage data</bold>: analytics events (guide views, paywall opens, frequency of use).
privacy.section3Body1: The data is processed for the following purposes:
privacy.section3Item1: operation of the Service (generation of action plans, dosage calculations, water monitoring);
privacy.section3Item2: improvement of AI models (photo diagnostic, recommendations) — based on prior anonymization when possible;
privacy.section3Item6: compliance with legal and accounting obligations.
privacy.section4Body1: In accordance with Article 6 of the GDPR, the processing is based on the following legal bases:
privacy.section4Item1: <bold>Contract performance</bold> (art. 6.1.b): provision of the subscribed Service.
privacy.section4Item2: <bold>Consent</bold> (art. 6.1.a): photos sent for diagnostic, notifications, possible marketing communications.
privacy.section4Item3: <bold>Legitimate interest</bold> (art. 6.1.f): Service improvement, security, fraud prevention.
privacy.section4Item4: <bold>Legal obligation</bold> (art. 6.1.c): retention of invoices and accounting records.
privacy.section4Body2: The User can withdraw their consent at any time from the <link>Settings</link> page, without affecting the lawfulness of the prior processing.
privacy.section5Item1: <bold>Active account</bold>: duration of use of the Service.
privacy.section5Item2: <bold>Pool data, measurements, photos</bold>: retained as long as the Account is active, plus 90 days after deletion (for possible restoration), then permanently erased.
privacy.section5Item5: <bold>Inactive account</bold>: after 24 months without login, notification by email then deletion of the Account and its data.
privacy.section6Body1: The data is accessible to the following recipients:
privacy.section6Item1: <bold>AQWELIA</bold>: authorized personnel (engineers, support) under confidentiality agreement.
privacy.section6Item3: <bold>Stripe</bold>: web payment processing (subject to its own GDPR).
privacy.section6Item5: <bold>Apple / Google</bold>: In-App payments (subject to their own terms).
privacy.section6Item6: <bold>AI providers</bold>: for photo diagnostic and the assistant — only strictly necessary data, with no storage beyond the session.
privacy.section6Body2: AQWELIA never sells your data to third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy.section7Body1: The main hosting is located in the European Union. Any possible transfer outside the EU (for example to an AI provider based in the United States) is framed by the standard contractual clauses of the European Commission (decision 2021/914) and accompanied by appropriate safeguards (encryption, minimization, pseudonymization).
privacy.section8Body1: In accordance with Articles 15 to 22 of the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.section8Item1: <bold>Right of access</bold>: obtain a copy of your data.
privacy.section8Item3: <bold>Right to erasure</bold> ("right to be forgotten"): delete your Account and your data.
privacy.section8Item4: <bold>Right to portability</bold>: receive your data in a structured and reusable format.
privacy.section8Item5: <bold>Right to object</bold>: refuse processing based on legitimate interest.
privacy.section8Item7: <bold>Right to withdraw your consent</bold> at any time.
privacy.section8Body2: These rights can be exercised directly from the <link>Settings</link> page (JSON export, account deletion), or by email at <alink>privacy@aqwelia.app</alink>.
privacy.section8Body3: You can also file a complaint with the CNIL (www.cnil.fr) or the supervisory authority of your country.
privacy.section9Body1: AQWELIA uses a minimum of trackers strictly necessary for the operation of the Service (authentication session, language and theme preferences). No advertising or third-party tracking cookies are deposited without consent.
privacy.section9Body2: Preferences can be managed from the <link>Settings</link> page.
privacy.section10Body1: AQWELIA implements appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data:
privacy.section10Item2: encryption of data in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (database encryption);
privacy.section10Item3: authentication via signed JWT, limited lifetime (30 days);
privacy.section10Item4: restricted access to personal data (principle of least privilege);
privacy.section10Item5: encrypted backups and regular penetration tests.
privacy.section10Body2: In the event of a data breach likely to result in a risk to your rights and freedoms, AQWELIA will notify you without undue delay in accordance with Article 34 of the GDPR.
privacy.section11Body1: For any question regarding the protection of your data, contact our DPO:
privacy.section11Body2: See also our <link>TOU</link> and our <link2>Support page</link2>.

test_overdue.title: Water test to redo
test_overdue.detail: Last test {days} days ago. Water quality changes fast.
test_overdue.action: Do a full test: pH + chlorine + TAC.

retest_product.title: Re-test after treatment
retest_product.detail: You recently added a product. Check the effect.
retest_product.action: Do another pH + chlorine test within the next few hours.

cell_clean.title: Electrolyzer cell cleaning
cell_clean.detail: The cell scales up over time. Acid cleaning every 3-6 months.
cell_clean.action: Remove the cell, soak in diluted acid solution 10-15 min, rinse.

skimmer_clean.title: Check skimmer and basket
skimmer_clean.detail: Debris, leaves, insects to remove regularly.
skimmer_clean.action: Empty the skimmer basket and the pump basket.

startup.title: Season start-up
startup.detail: Spring is coming: prepare the pool for the season.
startup.action: Follow the start-up guide: cleaning, treatment, filtration.

winterize.title: Prepare winterization
winterize.action: Follow the winterization guide: cold water, winter products, cover.

ph.label: pH
ph.consequenceLow: Acidic water: irritation, equipment corrosion, unstable chlorine.
ph.consequenceHigh: Basic water: less effective chlorine, scaling, cloudy water.

freeChlorine.label: Free chlorine
freeChlorine.consequenceLow: Insufficient disinfection: algae and bacteria risk.

combinedChlorine.label: Combined chlorine
combinedChlorine.consequenceHigh: High combined chlorine: strong odor, eye irritation, chloramines.

alkalinity.label: Alkalinity (TA)
alkalinity.consequenceHigh: pH hard to adjust, cloudy water.

cyanuricAcid.label: Stabilizer (CYA)
cyanuricAcid.consequenceHigh: Chlorine locked: ineffective treatment, algae risk.
